---
title: "Final"
author: "Isha Chhabra"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---


```{r}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(kableExtra)
library(here)
library(digest)
library(fs)
library(lme4)
library(tidyverse)
```

## Question 1
```{r}

#get player list code from assignment
response <- GET('https://api.chess.com/pub/country/US/players')

player_list <- fromJSON(rawToChar(response$content))$players
#from assignment for one username
info <- GET('https://api.chess.com/pub/player/--untouchable--')
fromJSON(rawToChar(info$content))
stats <- GET('https://api.chess.com/pub/player/--untouchable--/stats')
fromJSON(rawToChar(stats$content))

player_df <- function(username) {
  info  <- GET(paste0('https://api.chess.com/pub/player/', username))
  info  <- fromJSON(rawToChar(info$content))
  stats <- GET(paste0('https://api.chess.com/pub/player/', username, '/stats'))
  stats <- fromJSON(rawToChar(stats$content))
  tibble( #googled the tidyverse language and this came up %||%
    username          = username,
    name              = info$name                      %||% NA,
    followers         = info$followers                 %||% NA,
    location          = info$location                  %||% NA,
    last_online       = as.POSIXct(info$last_online, origin = "1970-01-01", tz = "UTC"), #from hint
    rapid_last_rating = stats$chess_rapid$last$rating  %||% NA,
    rapid_best_rating = stats$chess_rapid$best$rating  %||% NA,
    rapid_record_win  = stats$chess_rapid$record$win   %||% NA,
    rapid_record_loss = stats$chess_rapid$record$loss  %||% NA,
    rapid_record_draw = stats$chess_rapid$record$draw  %||% NA
  )
}

chess_df <- map_dfr(player_list[1:500], player_df) #week 4

chess_df |> 
  kable() |>
  kable_styling() #print(chess_df) looked ugly.. used styling to make it better
```
## Question 2
```{r}
data_q2 <- here("data", "FuncConn")

#list all CSV files- week 4 notes
all_files <- dir_ls(data_q2, regexp = "\\.csv$", recurse = TRUE) 
all_files <- all_files[grepl("2026-05", all_files)]

file_df <- tibble(
  path = all_files,
  hash = map_chr(all_files, tools::md5sum) #used claude to find out if two have same hash and how to identify: https://claude.ai/share/06cd9ab1-d150-4663-a6b4-d2e5526750a4
)
cat("Total files before:", length(all_files), "\n")
#keep original, group hash
duplicates_df <- file_df |>
  group_by(hash) |> #group files by MD5 hash
  filter(n() > 1) |> #keep only groups that have duplicates
  arrange(path) |> #sort alphabetically- organized
  mutate(is_original = row_number() == 1) |> #make the first file in each group original and rest duplicates
  ungroup() #remove grouping

originals  <- duplicates_df |> filter( is_original)
duplicates <- duplicates_df |> filter(!is_original) #TRUE to FALSE

#Delete duplicates from week 5 notes
walk(duplicates$path, file.remove)

#table
tibble(
  original_kept     = originals$path,
  duplicate_deleted = duplicates$path
) |> knitr::kable()

cat("Total files after:", length(list.files(data_q2, recursive = TRUE)), "\n")
```

## Question 3
```{r}
benchmarks <- read_csv(here("data", "benchmarks.csv"))
str(benchmarks)
#Recode season -> wave and change season values
benchmarks <- benchmarks |>
  mutate(wave = case_when(
    season == "fall"   ~ 0,
    season == "winter" ~ 1,
    season == "spring" ~ 2
  ))

#fit LME and compute slope reliability for one grade-subject subset- From week 6
slope_rel <- function(df, outcome) {
  # rename outcome column to score
  df <- rename(df, score = all_of(outcome))
  #numerator
  lme_fit <- suppressWarnings(
    lmer(score ~ 1 + wave + (1 + wave | sid), REML = FALSE, data = df)
  ) #from assignment- says to suppress warnings
  
  #true score var
  true_var <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(lme_fit)) |>
    filter(grp == "sid", var1 == "wave", is.na(var2)) |>
    pull(vcov) #used Claude to help with this code true_var: https://claude.ai/share/8b68cd78-2ed0-4299-925d-9cdafbbf4318
  
  #fit lm for each student - denominator
  obs_slopes <- split(df, df$sid) |> #from week 4 notes
    map_dbl(~coef(lm(score ~ 1 + wave, data = .x))[["wave"]]) #changed to just use wave variable from lm(score ~ 1+ wave, data = student)
  #observed score variance
  obs_var <- var(obs_slopes)
  
  #slope reliability formula
  as.numeric(true_var) / obs_var
}

#all grade and subject combo
results <- map_dfr(c(3, 4, 5), function(g) {
  map_dfr(c("reading", "math"), function(subj) {
    tibble(
      grade             = g,
      subject           = subj,
      slope_reliability = round(slope_rel(filter(benchmarks, grade == g), subj), 4)
    )
  })
})
results |> knitr::kable()

#results |> select(-model) |> knitr::kable()
```

## Question 4
```{r}
#refit model for Q4- Used Claude to help with models function: https://claude.ai/share/98603a5d-e6b5-49e9-a517-8e90f0b02d17
models <- map_dfr(c(3, 4, 5), function(g) {
  map_dfr(c("reading", "math"), function(subj) { #nested in 2 subjects for each grade from Week 4
    df <- benchmarks |> filter(grade == g) |> rename(score = all_of(subj)) #filter keeps only rows that grade. rename to score like before
    lme_fit <- suppressWarnings(
      lmer(score ~ 1 + wave + (1 + wave | sid), REML = FALSE, data = df) #from assignment
    )
    tibble( #create one row for each grade/subject combo
      grade   = g,
      subject = subj,
      model   = list(lme_fit)
    )
  })
})

slope_reliability <- function(lmer_obj) { #from week 6 slides
  true_var <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(lmer_obj)) |> #extract variance
    filter(grp != "Residual", var1 == "wave", is.na(var2)) |>
    pull(vcov) #extracts number for numerator
  
  mf     <- model.frame(lmer_obj) #name model frame that extracts data.. week 6
  mf$sid <- lmer_obj@flist[[1]] #pulls student ID's and add back to mf
  
  obs_slopes <- split(mf, mf$sid) |>
    map_dbl(~coef(lm(score ~ 1 + wave, data = .x))[["wave"]]) #week 4
  
  as.numeric(true_var) / var(obs_slopes)
}

models |>
  mutate(slope_reliability = round(map_dbl(model, ~slope_reliability(.x)), 4)) |>
  select(grade, subject, slope_reliability) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Question 5
```{r}
#Used claude twice to help with function and using safely: https://claude.ai/share/359d3313-1685-4958-b03f-a20f0fe49c1f
#bootstrap- week 5 and 6 notes
bootstrap_samples <- function(df, id_var, time_var, outcome_var, n_boot) {
  ids <- unique(df[[id_var]]) #unique IDs
  n   <- length(ids) #count students
  #week 5 maps notes
  map(seq_len(n_boot), function(i) { #Week 5
    sampled_ids <- ids[base::sample(seq_len(n), size = n, replace = TRUE)] #sample with replacement (from notes in my other data class)
    
    #map2_dfr to bind rows week 4
    map2_dfr(sampled_ids, seq_along(sampled_ids), function(id, idx) {
      df |>
        filter(.data[[id_var]] == id) |> #week 7, retain fall, winter, spring courses
        mutate("{id_var}" := paste0(id, "_", idx))
    }) #makes sure lmer doesn't get confused with bootstrapping- renames duplicate students
  })
}

#slope reliability from q4- except takes in raw df and fits to model
slope_rel_from_data <- function(df, id_var, time_var, outcome_var) {
  
  # rename to match lmer formula
  df <- rename(df, score = all_of(outcome_var))
  
  #numerator
  lme_fit <- suppressWarnings(
    lmer(score ~ 1 + wave + (1 + wave | sid), REML = FALSE, data = df) #from assignment
  )
  
  true_var <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(lme_fit)) |>
    filter(grp == id_var, var1 == time_var, is.na(var2)) |>
    pull(vcov)
  
  #denominator- week 4 slides
  obs_slopes <- split(df, df[[id_var]]) |>
    map_dbl(~coef(lm(score ~ 1 + wave, data = .x))[["wave"]])
  
  as.numeric(true_var) / var(obs_slopes)
}

safe_slope_rel <- safely(slope_rel_from_data) #week 5
# run bootstraps for each grade x subject
set.seed(2026) #don't need but wanted to keep to check (reproducible)
n_boot <- 100

ci_results <- map_dfr(c(3, 4, 5), function(g) {
  map_dfr(c("reading", "math"), function(subj) {
    
    df_sub <- benchmarks |> filter(grade == g) #filter grade at time
    
    #100 bootstrap samples
    boots <- bootstrap_samples(df_sub, "sid", "wave", subj, n_boot)
    
    #week 4: map_dbl() to apply function to list
      boot_rels <- map(boots, ~safe_slope_rel(.x, "sid", "wave", subj)) |>
        map("result") |> #week 5
        compact() |>
        map_dbl(~.x)
    
    tibble(
      grade   = g,
      subject = subj,
      n       = n_distinct(df_sub$sid),
      ci_lower           = round(quantile(boot_rels, 0.025), 4),
      ci_upper           = round(quantile(boot_rels, 0.975), 4),
      median_reliability = round(median(boot_rels), 4)
    )
  })
})

ci_results |>
  knitr::kable(col.names = c("Grade", "Subject", "N", 
                              "95% CI Lower", "95% CI Upper", 
                              "Median Reliability"))
```
